narratives.evidence_quote: रूस ने साफ-साफ कह दिया है कि अगर नाटो यूक्रेन के समर्थन में उसके शहरों पर हमला करता है तो परमाणु युद्ध टाला नहीं जा सकता.
narratives.reasoning: This quote directly evokes fear by stating that a NATO attack on Russian cities would lead to unavoidable nuclear war, creating anxiety about catastrophic escalation.

narratives.evidence_quote: रक्षा एक्सपर्ट्स का मानना है कि अगर यूक्रेन इसी तरह से हमला करता रहा तो अगले साल मार्च-अप्रैल तक ये बेहद कमजोर हो जाएगा.
narratives.reasoning: This statement predicts a specific future outcome of the conflict, speculating that Ukraine will become 'extremely weak' by a certain date if it continues its current actions.

narratives.evidence_quote: यह पश्चिमी देशों को अपनी ताकत दिखाने का एक तरीका है, ताकि जंग को रोका जा सके.
narratives.reasoning: This frames a potential Russian nuclear test as a demonstration of strength and a rational method to prevent war, portraying Russia's actions as powerful and strategically sound.

narratives.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narratives.evidence_quote: अगर रूस इन केबल्स को काटता है तो इसका असर पश्चिमी देशों समेत कई देशों की अर्थव्यवस्था पर पड़ेगा.
narratives.reasoning: This highlights a potential adverse outcome specifically for Western countries, predicting economic damage if Russia acts against undersea internet cables.

narratives.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narratives.evidence_quote: इसलिए कुर्स्क इलाके में नाटो के सपोर्ट से हमला किया गया.
narratives.reasoning: This statement attributes the initiation of an attack in the Kursk region to NATO's support, shifting blame for military escalation away from Russia and onto NATO/Ukraine.

narratives.evidence_quote: रूस ने कहा कि यूक्रेन के सहारे अगर नाटो रूस के शहरों पर हमला करने की हिमाकत करता है, तो ये बेवकूफी होगी.
narratives.reasoning: This portrays Russia as a potential victim of a foolish NATO/Ukrainian attack on its cities, framing Russia as the party reacting to external aggression.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: By continuing the war we risk WWIII
subnarratives.evidence_quote: रूस ने साफ-साफ कह दिया है कि अगर नाटो यूक्रेन के समर्थन में उसके शहरों पर हमला करता है तो परमाणु युद्ध टाला नहीं जा सकता.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly warns that NATO's support for Ukraine could lead to a nuclear war, evoking fear of a global escalation and World War III as a consequence of continuing the conflict.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: There is a real possibility that nuclear weapons will be employed
subnarratives.evidence_quote: इसके लिए रूस टैक्टिकल न्यूक्लियर वेपन यानी परमाणु हथियारों का इस्तेमाल कर सकता है.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement explicitly presents the use of tactical nuclear weapons by Russia as a plausible option in the conflict, directly amplifying fears about nuclear escalation.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: NATO should/will directly intervene
subnarratives.evidence_quote: रूस के राष्ट्रपति व्लादिमीर पुतिन ने स्पष्ट कहा है कि अगर नाटो ने सीधा हमला किया तो जंग रूस और नाटो की होगी.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote frames the conflict as potentially shifting to a direct war between Russia and NATO, implying that NATO's direct intervention is a realistic and dangerous possibility.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: रूस समंदर के अंदर खोज रहा है इंटरनेट केबल... अगर रूस इन केबल्स को काटता है तो इसका असर पश्चिमी देशों समेत कई देशों की अर्थव्यवस्था पर पड़ेगा.
subnarratives.reasoning: This describes a non-nuclear, asymmetric threat (attacking undersea internet cables) that Russia could employ to cause widespread economic damage, amplifying war-related fears through a specific, non-conventional escalation scenario not covered by the other subnarratives.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Speculating war outcomes: Ukrainian army is collapsing
subnarratives.evidence_quote: रक्षा एक्सपर्ट्स का मानना है कि अगर यूक्रेन इसी तरह से हमला करता रहा तो अगले साल मार्च-अप्रैल तक ये बेहद कमजोर हो जाएगा।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly speculates on the future outcome of the war, predicting a significant decline in Ukrainian military strength and effectiveness ('बेहद कमजोर हो जाएगा'), which aligns with the definition of the subnarrative about the Ukrainian army collapsing.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Praise of Russia: Praise of Russian military might
subnarratives.evidence_quote: रूस ने इस बीच दोनेत्सक इलाके में काफी बार सफलता हासिल की है। रक्षा एक्सपर्ट्स का मानना है कि अगर यूक्रेन इसी तरह से हमला करता रहा तो अगले साल मार्च-अप्रैल तक ये बेहद कमजोर हो जाएगा।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote highlights Russia's military successes on the battlefield (Donetsk) and presents an expert assessment that Ukraine will be severely weakened, indirectly praising the effectiveness and might of the Russian military campaign.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Praise of Russia: Russia is a guarantor of peace and prosperity
subnarratives.evidence_quote: यह परीक्षण किसी भी समय हो सकता है। यह पश्चिमी देशों को अपनी ताकत दिखाने का एक तरीका है, ताकि जंग को रोका जा सके।
subnarratives.reasoning: The text frames Russia's potential nuclear test not as aggression but as a demonstration of strength intended to 'stop the war,' portraying Russia as an actor using its power to prevent further conflict and thus guarantee peace.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Praise of Russia: Russia has international support from a number of countries and people
subnarratives.evidence_quote: उसे चीन और रूस का समर्थन हासिल है। वह भी यूक्रेन को हराने में रूस का साथ दे रहा है। साथ ही यह भी कहा है कि अगर जरूरत पड़ी तो वह सीधे तौर पर रूस के साथ खड़ा होगा।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement explicitly mentions that North Korea has the support of China and Russia, and that North Korea is supporting Russia in defeating Ukraine, even ready to stand directly with Russia, emphasizing Russia's international backing.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: अमेरिका में मौजूद रूस के एंबेसडर ने अमेरिका को चेतावनी दी है कि ये दूसरा विश्व युद्ध नहीं है कि अमेरिकी पनडुब्बियां पानी के अंदर से आकर अचानक हमला कर देंगी। न उसमें छिप पाएंगी। हम हर जगह से नजर रखे हुए हैं।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote praises Russia's strategic vigilance and omnipresent surveillance capabilities, presenting it as a formidable and prepared power. It supports the parent narrative of praising Russia but does not fit neatly into the more specific subnarrative definitions provided (e.g., it's not solely about military might, Putin, peace, or support).

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Sanctions imposed by Western countries will backfire
subnarratives.evidence_quote: अगर रूस इन केबल्स को काटता है तो इसका असर पश्चिमी देशों समेत कई देशों की अर्थव्यवस्था पर पड़ेगा।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly states that a potential Russian action (cutting undersea internet cables) would negatively impact the economies of Western countries, framing a Russian countermeasure as having damaging consequences for the West.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: अगर नाटो ने सीधा हमला किया तो जंग रूस और नाटो की होगी।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement, along with the entire article's focus on escalating the conflict to a direct NATO-Russia war and the threat of nuclear weapons, supports the parent narrative by catastrophizing the negative consequences (a major war) for the West if it continues its support for Ukraine. This is a broader consequence not captured by the specific 'sanctions backfire' or 'refugee flows' subnarratives.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: The West are the aggressors
subnarratives.evidence_quote: रूस ने साफ-साफ कह दिया है कि अगर नाटो यूक्रेन के समर्थन में उसके शहरों पर हमला करता है तो परमाणु युद्ध टाला नहीं जा सकता. रूस ने कहा कि यूक्रेन के सहारे अगर नाटो रूस के शहरों पर हमला करने की हिमाकत करता है, तो ये बेवकूफी होगी.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote frames NATO as the potential aggressor that could attack Russian cities, shifting the blame for escalation and conflict away from Russia's invasion and onto the Western alliance.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: Ukraine is the aggressor
subnarratives.evidence_quote: रक्षा एक्सपर्ट्स का मानना है कि अगर यूक्रेन इसी तरह से हमला करता रहा तो अगले साल मार्च-अप्रैल तक ये बेहद कमजोर हो जाएगा. इसलिए कुर्स्क इलाके में नाटो के सपोर्ट से हमला किया गया.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement portrays Ukraine as the continuing attacker ('if Ukraine keeps attacking like this') and frames its actions in Kursk as an offensive operation, thereby shifting responsibility for the conflict onto Ukraine.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: यह पश्चिमी देशों को अपनी ताकत दिखाने का एक तरीका है, ताकि जंग को रोका जा सके.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote suggests that Russia's potential nuclear test is a defensive measure to 'stop the war,' implying that the war's continuation is due to Western actions, which is a form of blame-shifting that doesn't neatly fit the more specific subnarratives.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Russia is the Victim: Russia actions in Ukraine are only self-defence
subnarratives.evidence_quote: रूस ने साफ-साफ कह दिया है कि अगर नाटो यूक्रेन के समर्थन में उसके शहरों पर हमला करता है तो परमाणु युद्ध टाला नहीं जा सकता. रूस ने कहा कि यूक्रेन के सहारे अगर नाटो रूस के शहरों पर हमला करने की हिमाकत करता है, तो ये बेवकूफी होगी.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote frames Russia's potential actions, including nuclear retaliation, as a defensive response to a hypothetical NATO/Ukraine attack on Russian cities. It presents Russia as merely reacting to external aggression, justifying its stance as self-defence.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Russia is the Victim: The West is russophobic
subnarratives.evidence_quote: यह पश्चिमी देशों को अपनी ताकत दिखाने का एक तरीका है, ताकि जंग को रोका जा सके.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement, referring to a potential Russian nuclear test, suggests that Russia's show of force is necessary to deter Western countries, implying that the West is the aggressive party pushing for conflict, which aligns with the idea of Western hostility (russophobia) driving the situation.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Russia is the Victim: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: इसलिए कुर्स्क इलाके में नाटो के सपोर्ट से हमला किया गया.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement directly attributes an attack on Russian territory (Kursk region) to NATO support for Ukraine. It supports the overarching 'Russia is the Victim' narrative by portraying Russia as under attack from the West/NATO, but it does not fit neatly into the more specific subnarratives of 'self-defence' (as it describes an attack on Russia), 'russophobia' (it's a specific action, not a general attitude), or 'UA extremists' (it blames NATO).
